title: eriogonum latifolium subsp. auriculatum
intro: Eriogonum latifolium subsp. auriculatum, also known as a variant of the Sulphur Buckwheat or Sulphur Buckwheat Buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States and British Columbia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Eriogonum latifolium subsp. auriculatum can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical applications, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
